label: Ship It
subject: Shipping code (a build pipeline)
bossHook: Take a feature from an empty repo all the way to production — in an order that actually works.

order-matters.title: First Things First
order-matters.hook: You can't deploy code you haven't written. Put these three in an order that works.
order-matters.winText: Repo → code → deploy. Order matters: each step depends on the one before it.

prerequisite.title: The Foundation
prerequisite.hook: Some steps unlock others. Find the one thing that has to happen first, then build on it.
prerequisite.winText: The repo came first — code and docs both needed it. That's a prerequisite.

failText.wrong-start: You started with a step that needs something else first. Begin with what needs nothing — the repo.
failText.dependency-violation: That step ran before its prerequisite existed. Put the thing it needs earlier.

the-chain.title: The Whole Chain
the-chain.hook: Write code, then test AND review it, then merge. Some steps wait on others; trace the chain.
the-chain.winText: Tests and review both waited on the code, and the merge waited on both. You traced the whole chain.

failText.wrong-start: Start with the step that needs nothing.
failText.dependency-violation: You merged or tested before the code it depends on existed.

boss-pipeline.title: BOSS · Ship the Feature
boss-pipeline.hook: Six steps, two branches that must converge. Build the whole pipeline from an empty repo to production.
boss-pipeline.winText: Feature shipped. Code and tests both branched off the repo, then converged at the deploy. That's a real pipeline.

failText.wrong-start: Begin with the step that needs nothing — the repo.
failText.dependency-violation: A step ran before something it depends on. Trace what each one needs.

missing-foundation.title: SIDE-QUEST · Foundation First
missing-foundation.hook: You keep building before the base exists. Drill it: place the ONE step everything else needs, first.
missing-foundation.winText: Repo first, then the rest. The foundation is whatever needs nothing and unlocks everything.

failText.wrong-start: Still starting in the wrong place — lead with the step that has no prerequisites.
failText.dependency-violation: Put the foundation before the steps that depend on it.
